objective: Observe the smallest finite official outlet-to-inlet connection setup on local loopback through documented public APIs, retaining raw transport/XML/endpoint/diagnostic evidence privately and publishing only provenance-locked public-safe message-role, bounded framing, typed outcome, cleanup, and limitation facts sufficient to assess one later minimal Rust handshake vertical slice.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the bounded official stream-handshake observation and strict nonclaims.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Run handshake provenance/privacy and owner gates.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Separate observed connection setup from an implemented Rust handshake or sample path.
Record exact direction, behavior, limitations, and evidence role.
Bind official artifacts and independently authored black-box drivers without source intake.
Route bounded replay, cleanup, and private raw-evidence handling.
Review existing black-box and public/private guidance; no shared-skill content change expected.
Review existing behavioral-evidence and authority guidance; no shared-skill content change expected.

non_scope: Rust outlet/inlet implementation, listener or connector ownership, non-loopback networking, discovery changes, multicast, interface selection, retries, scheduling, persistence, or background runtime.
Sample bytes or transport, clock exchange/correction, buffering, backpressure, recovery, performance, currentness, reachability, multiple hosts, or ecosystem-wide interoperability.
Implementation-source inspection, copied code, public raw packets/XML/endpoints/diagnostics, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe/FFI, devices, private artifacts in Git, or Manifold authority.

acceptance.proof: The observation binds exact official package/library identities and only documented public APIs without inspecting implementation source.

At least two finite synthetic cases establish the minimal observed outlet/inlet connection setup roles, typed outcomes, explicit timeout, and deterministic process/socket cleanup without sending samples.
Only public-safe derived message/framing roles and pass/fail facts are committed; raw transport, XML, endpoint/runtime values, diagnostics, binaries, environments, and caches remain private.
The record explicitly grants no Rust handshake/sample implementation, endpoint authority, reachability, currentness, performance, broad interoperability, or Manifold authority.

outputs: Public-safe derived official outlet/inlet connection-setup observation matrix.
Pinned provenance/privacy/limitation checker and synchronized compatibility routing.
Private external raw observation and failed-attempt bundle retained outside Git.
commit_policy: Commit only sanitized derived evidence and routing after bounded private observation; keep raw artifacts external and lifecycle/acceptance/push commits separate.
